comments.body: Try with distributionUrl=https\://services.gradle.org/distributions/g‌​radle-7.5.1-bin.zip. If it doesn't work, try a lower minor version e.g. gradle-7.2-all.zip or choose another from services.gradle.org/distributions
What happens if you try on the command line, without IntelliJ? What does ./gradlew assemble, java --version, and echo $JAVA_HOME output?

My project works with Gradle 7.3.1 and JDK 17 on 2022.3 IDE version. Can you provide the sample project? What IDE version do you use? Also please clarify how does it work from the command line with the same JDK and Gradle versions.

answers.body:
Follw below steps to solve this issue :

  1. Click on Android studio setting option
  2. click on Build, Execution, Deployment
  3. Click on Build tools
  4. Click on Gradle
  5. check the gradle JDK is available or not
  6. If you click on option of Gradle JDK dropdown else get the option that you will get on botton Download JDK
  7. According to your requirment kindly download the JDK

title: Java Gradle Error : Your build is currently configured to use Java 17.0.2 and Gradle 7.3.1
body:
We are attempting to upgrade our project to use Java 17 with a compatible Gradle. We expect that Java 17.0.2 and Gradle 7.3.1 should be compatible per Gradle Compatibility Matrix

However, when we attempt to run our project we get the following error :

Unsupported Java. 
Your build is currently configured to use Java 17.0.2 and Gradle 7.3.1.

Possible solution:
 - Use Java 16 as Gradle JVM: Open Gradle settings 
 - Open Gradle wrapper settings, change `distributionUrl` property to use compatible Gradle version and reload the project

We are using IntelliJ with Gradle JVM set to : openjdk-17 version 17.0.2 and Project SDK set to : openjdk-17 version 17.0.2

We are using Gradle distribution url : distributionUrl=https\://services.gradle.org/distributions/gradle-7.3.1-all.zip

It appears that these should be compatible does anyone know what could be causing this?
